title: Marguerite Lenentine (née Danforth): A Waldo County Daughter Who Married into the Lenentine Family (c.1896–1976)
narrative: Marguerite Danforth was born about 1896 in Maine, a daughter of Otis B. Danforth and Gertrude Evelyn Bridges. Her childhood unfolded in a Maine that was still largely rural and agricultural at the turn of the twentieth century, in a region of small farming towns and tight-knit communities. The Danforth and Bridges surnames were long established in Maine by her parents' generation, placing Marguerite among the native Yankee families of the state. The precise town of her birth is not confirmed in the free records consulted here, so we describe her origins honestly as a Maine girl of the Danforth line rather than pinning her to a single village.

As a young woman she married Guy L. Lenentine — recorded in family sources as Guy Lester Lenentine — joining a family whose roots reached across the border into New Brunswick, Canada. The Lenentines were one of the families woven through the Maine–New Brunswick borderland, with a strong and enduring concentration in Aroostook County, Maine, and across the line in southern New Brunswick. To this day the family's connection to that corner of Maine is marked by an annual Lenentine reunion held on Labor Day weekend at the Monticello Grange Hall near Houlton. Through her marriage, Marguerite became part of that cross-border world, bridging the old Yankee Danforth family of Maine and the Lenentine kin of the St. John River country.

The couple's life appears to have centered on Monticello, in Aroostook County near the New Brunswick frontier, where their children were born. Their daughter Grace was born there on May 12, 1920, and a published obituary for Grace records the family circle in detail: besides Grace, Marguerite and Guy raised at least three more children — Halston Lenentine, Helen (Lenentine) Bernier, and Phil Lenentine. This makes Marguerite the mother of a household of at least four, and her descendants — including grandchildren who carried the family forward in towns such as Troy and Belfast, Maine — trace their line directly back to her.

The era she lived through, roughly 1896 to 1976, spanned extraordinary change for an ordinary Maine family. She was born into a horse-and-woodstove world of kerosene lamps and farm labor; she lived through two world wars, the influenza pandemic of 1918, the Great Depression, and the slow arrival of automobiles, electricity, and modern roads into northern and central Maine. A woman of her generation in rural Maine would have carried the heavy daily work of keeping a farm household — gardening, preserving, cooking, sewing, and raising children — while also being knit into the church, grange, and neighborly networks that held small Maine towns together.

By the end of her life Marguerite had left Aroostook County for Waldo County in central Maine, the heart of the region into which several of her descendants settled. She died in May 1976 in Unity, a small Waldo County town. The move south is consistent with the family's later center of gravity around Unity, Troy, and Belfast, where her daughter Grace's family lived and farmed. We do not have a verified free record of her exact burial place, so we leave that point open rather than guess.

Much of Marguerite's inner life — her personality, her faith, the texture of her days — is not preserved in the free public record, and we will not invent it. What can be documented is the shape of a long Maine life: a Danforth daughter born around 1896, married to Guy Lester Lenentine, mother of Grace, Halston, Helen, and Phil, rooted first in the Maine–New Brunswick borderland at Monticello and ending her days in Unity in 1976. Hers is the quiet, well-attested story of a woman who joined two families and two sides of a border, and whose descendants still gather and remember.

sources.title: Grace L. Bagley Obituary (1920–2017), Bangor Daily News — names parents Guy Lester Lenentine and Marguerite (Danforth) Lenentine, birth in Monticello, and siblings Halston, Helen Bernier, and Phil Lenentine
Monticello, Aroostook County, Maine Genealogy — FamilySearch Research Wiki (records and context for the Lenentine family's home town)
Unity, Waldo County, Maine Genealogy — FamilySearch Research Wiki (context for Marguerite's place of death)
Cemeteries in Unity, Maine — Find a Grave (context for Waldo County burials)
